headline: Berkshire’s new CEO Abel says he talks with Buffett nearly every day
summary: Investing.com -- Berkshire Hathaway new CEO Greg Abel said he still speaks with Warren Buffett nearly every day, highlighting the continued involvement of the legendary investor at the conglomerate even after stepping down from the top position at the start of the year.

headline: Berkshire's Greg Abel Buys Company Shares, Restarts Share Buybacks
summary: The approximate value of Berkshire Hathaway stock that new CEO Greg Abel personally bought, according to a regulatory filing.  Speaking on CNBC Thursday morning, Abel said he used his entire take-home pay to buy Berkshire shares, and that he plans to do this every year he is CEO to show "absolute alignment with our shareholders."  Abel, Warren Buffett's hand-picked successor, took over as Berkshire's CEO in January.

headline: Greg Abel Just Bought $15 Million of Warren Buffett’s Favorite Stock
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK-A)(NYSE:BRK-B) is in a new era. After decades at the helm, Warren Buffett stepped back from day-to-day control, handing the CEO reins to Greg Abel at the start of 2026. Investors and analysts have watched closely to see how faithfully Abel would follow Buffett’s legendary playbook of patience, discipline, and opportunistic capital allocation. ... summary: Greg Abel has taken over as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A), succeeding Warren Buffett in the company’s most significant leadership change in decades. Abel has begun personally purchasing Berkshire shares and has authorized fresh company share buybacks. He has emphasized continuity with Buffett’s long standing principles while consulting closely with him during the transition. Berkshire Hathaway is a diversified holding company with businesses spanning insurance, energy, railroads,...

headline: Berkshire Hathaway resumes buybacks and CEO supports Kraft's decision to pause its split
summary: Berkshire Hathaway is buying back shares for the first time in nearly two years, and new CEO Greg Abel said he has no immediate plans to sell off Kraft Heinz shares now that the packaged food giant has shelved its plan to split the company into two.  Abel appeared on CNBC Thursday — less than a week after releasing his first letter to shareholders after taking over the top job at Berkshire from legendary investor Warren Buffett in January.  Berkshire also took the unusual step of filing a formal notice with the Securities and Exchange Commission that it had begun repurchasing its shares Wednesday for the first time since May 2024.

content.title: Berkshire Hathaway resumes buybacks and CEO supports Kraft's decision to pause its split

Abel appeared on CNBC Thursday — less than a week after releasing his first letter to shareholders after taking over the top job at Berkshire from legendary investor Warren Buffett in January.  Berkshire also took the unusual step of filing a formal notice with the Securities and Exchange Commission that it had begun repurchasing its shares Wednesday for the first time since May 2024.
